name: Automation Workflow System
professionalExpectations: An automation workflow system must be modular, observable, reversible, and explicit about every trigger and side effect.
Workflow activation, connector credentials, production data access, and external messaging are gated by default.
n8n workflows must be designed as small units with backup, rollback, and disabled-by-default behavior before activation.
The first version should prove the model with offline records and dry-run steps.
minimumQualityBar: Triggers, inputs, transformations, outputs, retries, failure handling, and rollback are documented.
No workflow is activated and no connector action is called without approval.
Each side effect has an owner-approved gate, audit event requirement, and disable path.
The workflow can be understood from records without relying on hidden UI state.
commonModules: Workflow inventory, trigger catalog, input/output schema, and side-effect map.
Dry-run executor, validation checks, audit trail, retry policy, and failure classification.
Connector gate records for GitHub, n8n, Netlify, Postgres, messaging, and future services.
Backup, rollback, disable, and incident-response instructions.
uxExpectations: Workflow views should expose status, trigger, next run, last result, owner, and risk.
Side effects must be visible before any execution command is available.
Error states should explain what failed, what was skipped, and what requires approval.
Operators should be able to find a disable or rollback path quickly.

components: Local workflow records for first modeling
n8n only after approval, backup, and disabled-first design
GitHub for versioned workflow definitions and change review
Existing Postgres only after approved data handling
Netlify only for approved read-only visibility
recommendedStack.notes: Do not activate workflows during archetype or dry-run phases. Design for backup and rollback before live execution.

phases.name: Workflow Map
goals: Define trigger, inputs, outputs, side effects, approvals, and rollback.
Classify connector, data, and cost risks.

phases.name: Dry-Run Records
goals: Create local execution records and audit events without connector calls.
Validate failure states and blocked side effects.

phases.name: Approved Activation
goals: Activate one modular workflow only after approval, backup, rollback, and monitoring readiness.
Record audit events for activation, execution, and rollback readiness.

knownPitfalls: Activating workflows before dry-run behavior and rollback are proven.
Combining too many side effects in one workflow.
Relying on service UI configuration that is not backed up or documented.
Creating hidden costs or external messages through connector actions.
approvalGates: Owner approval is required before workflow activation, connector credentials, live API calls, paid actions, or external messages.
Owner approval is required before production data, customer data, Postgres, n8n, Netlify, domain, DNS, or deployment changes.
Owner approval is required before destructive actions, scheduling recurring jobs, or enabling retries against live systems.
Owner approval and backup evidence are required before modifying production workflows.
qualityChecklist: Trigger, input, output, side effects, retries, and failure states are documented.
Workflow records are modular and versionable.
Dry-run behavior creates audit evidence without live action.
Backup, disable, rollback, cost, and incident paths are documented.
whatNotToOverbuildEarly: Multi-step live automation before one workflow is safe and observable.
Complex scheduling, retries, or branching before failure handling is clear.
Connector credentials or live service calls during planning.
Production workflow edits without a restore point.
stopConditions: Stop before n8n activation, connector execution, live API use, paid service use, external messaging, credentials, or production data handling.
Stop before deployment, domain or DNS change, destructive migration, or recurring job scheduling.
Stop before modifying existing Lead Gen workflows or AI Receptionist assets without separate approval.
notes: Archetype knowledge record only. It does not activate workflows or authorize live connector use.
